_comment: Single source of truth for strategy state. Edit manually or run: python agents/strategy_tracker.py --log. The dashboard reads this live.

current.name: GoldBot — Phase 3
current.phase_summary: Trend-following on XAUUSD. AI confirms entries after 5 pre-filters eliminate bad setups. ATR-locked 2:1 risk-reward on every trade.

this_week.focus: Validating ADX filter effectiveness — checking if ADX > 20 gate is correctly removing choppy/ranging markets before AI is called.
this_week.hypothesis: Removing ranging markets should lift win rate above 60% on trending sessions.
this_week.watch_metric: Win rate on London + Overlap sessions

filters.name: Session Gate
filters.plain_english: The bot only trades during the London session (08:00–13:00 UTC) and the London/New York overlap (13:00–16:00 UTC). Outside these hours the gold market is too quiet or too thin to trade reliably.
filters.why: These two sessions have the highest trading volume for gold, which means prices move more cleanly and are less likely to reverse randomly.
filters.threshold: Allowed: london, overlap — Blocked: asian, new_york, dead

filters.name: ADX Trend Filter
filters.plain_english: ADX measures how strongly the market is trending versus just moving sideways. If the score is below 20, the market is choppy and the bot skips the cycle.
filters.why: Trend-following strategies lose money in sideways markets. ADX below 20 means no clear direction — any signal is likely a false alarm.
filters.blocks_approx: ~20% of cycles that passed session gate

filters.name: EMA Alignment Gate
filters.plain_english: Two moving averages (EMA 20 and EMA 50) are checked on both the 1-hour and 4-hour charts. The bot only trades if both timeframes agree on direction. If they disagree, it waits.
filters.why: Requiring H1+H4 agreement ensures the bot trades with momentum across timeframes, not against a higher-timeframe trend.
filters.threshold: EMA20 > EMA50 on both H1 and H4 (bullish), or both bearish

filters.name: Price Change Gate
filters.plain_english: If price hasn't moved at least 30% of the average candle range (ATR) since the last AI call, the bot skips — nothing new has happened.
filters.why: Prevents calling the AI on an unchanged market, which wastes API budget and risks duplicate trades.
filters.threshold: Price must move ≥ 0.3 × H1 ATR since last AI call

filters.name: Post-Trade Cooldown
filters.plain_english: After any trade is placed, the bot pauses for 5 minutes before considering another one.
filters.why: Prevents entering multiple trades on the same market move, which would multiply risk beyond the 1% limit.

filters.name: AI Confidence Threshold
filters.plain_english: The AI must be at least 65% confident before a trade is placed. Below 65%, the bot logs the decision but does not execute.
filters.why: Low-confidence AI decisions perform similarly to random entries. Only higher-confidence calls extract the AI's edge.

indicators.name: RSI (Relative Strength Index)
indicators.role: Sent to AI as context — not a hard filter
indicators.plain_english: Measures whether Gold has been bought too much (overbought, above 70) or sold too much (oversold, below 30). The AI uses this to judge whether a move has room to continue or is likely to reverse.
indicators.what_to_watch: Extreme RSI values (< 30 or > 70) on H4 or D1 often precede strong reversals.

indicators.name: EMA 20 / EMA 50
indicators.role: Hard filter (EMA alignment gate) + AI context
indicators.plain_english: Moving averages that smooth price noise to show the underlying trend. EMA 20 reacts faster, EMA 50 is slower. When EMA 20 is above EMA 50 on both H1 and H4, the trend is up.
indicators.what_to_watch: A fresh EMA crossover (20 crossing 50) on H4 is one of the strongest signals.

indicators.name: ATR (Average True Range)
indicators.plain_english: Measures how much Gold typically moves in a single candle. Used to set the stop loss (1× ATR below entry) and take profit (2× ATR above entry) — automatically adjusting to current volatility.
indicators.what_to_watch: Very high ATR (>$15) means large position risk. H1 ATR below $2 is treated as bad data and ignored.

indicators.name: ADX (Average Directional Index)
indicators.plain_english: Scores the strength of a trend from 0 to 100. Below 20 means no real trend. Above 25 means a strong trend. The bot only trades when ADX is above 20.
indicators.what_to_watch: ADX rising from below 20 to above 25 on H1 is a good early trend signal.

entry_logic: All pre-filters must pass (session, ADX ≥ 20, EMA alignment H1+H4, price change gate, cooldown)
AI (LLaMA 3.3 70B via Groq) receives H1, H4, D1 data — RSI, EMA, ATR, ADX + last 5 candles per timeframe
AI returns BUY, SELL, or HOLD with a confidence score and plain-English reason
If confidence ≥ 65% and action is BUY or SELL, the trade is placed
Stop loss = 1× H1 ATR from entry. Take profit = 2× H1 ATR. Minimum 2:1 risk-reward locked in.
exit_logic: Phase 1 — Break-even: when profit reaches 1× original SL distance, stop loss moves to entry price (worst case = zero loss)
Phase 2 — ATR trail: once break-even fires, SL trails 1× ATR behind current price to lock in profits
Take profit: trade closes automatically at 2× ATR target
Stop loss: trade closes automatically if price reverses to SL level
risk_rules: 1% of account balance risked per trade
Maximum 2 open trades at any time
3% daily drawdown limit — bot stops trading for the day if hit
known_issues: Session filter blocks New York session entirely — may miss strong momentum after London close
ADX threshold of 20 may be too conservative — some good trending setups filtered. Consider testing 17.
AI is not aware of major news events (CPI, NFP) — sharp news moves can hit stop losses

disabled_ideas.name: News Block via ForexFactory API
disabled_ideas.reason: Not implemented — would block trading 30 min before/after high-impact news

future_plans.title: News Event Filter
future_plans.description: Block trading 30 min before and after high-impact news events (CPI, NFP, FOMC) by querying ForexFactory API. This should reduce stop-loss hits on sudden news spikes.

future_plans.title: Lower ADX Threshold Test
future_plans.description: Test ADX threshold at 17 instead of 20 to see if more trades are captured without sacrificing win rate. Run for 1 week and compare session win rates.

future_plans.title: New York Session Pilot
future_plans.description: Add New York session (16:00–21:00 UTC) as an allowed session but only on trending days (D1 ADX > 25). Currently all NY trades are blocked.

future_plans.title: Confidence-Scaled Lot Size
future_plans.description: Scale position size with AI confidence — 0.5× lot at 65-75%, 1× at 75-85%, 1.5× at 85%+. Currently lot size is fixed by risk % regardless of confidence.

history.title: Phase 3 — ADX filter, 5-candle context, AI call pre-filters
history.summary: Added ADX trend filter to block ranging markets. Expanded AI context to last 5 candles per timeframe. Added price-change gate and post-trade cooldown to reduce unnecessary AI calls.

changes.item: ADX filter — blocks when H1 ADX < 20 (ranging market)
5-candle OHLC context per timeframe sent to AI
Price change gate — skips AI if price moved < 0.3× ATR
Post-trade cooldown — 5 min pause after any order
EMA alignment now requires H1 AND H4 agreement (was H1-only)

history.outcome_note: Currently running — outcome pending enough trades to evaluate

history.title: Phase 2 — Live chart, session filter, ATR-locked SL/TP
history.summary: Added session filter restricting trading to London and Overlap only. Replaced fixed-dollar SL/TP with ATR-locked sizing for automatic volatility adjustment. Added live Plotly candlestick chart to dashboard.

changes.item: Session filter — london and overlap sessions only
ATR-locked SL/TP — 1× ATR stop, 2× ATR target (2:1 RR guaranteed)
Break-even stop — moves SL to entry when profit = 1× SL distance
ATR trailing stop — trails 1× ATR after break-even fires

history.outcome_note: Replaced by Phase 3. Win rate was ~52% — acceptable but ADX was not filtering ranging periods.

history.title: Phase 1 — Performance measurement and trade exit tracking
history.summary: Added trade exit tracking to the database, P&L logging, and the first performance metrics to the dashboard. Bot could now measure its own performance over time.

changes.item: Trade exit tracking — pnl_dollars, exit_price, close_reason, duration logged
Win rate, avg P&L, session stats on dashboard

history.outcome_note: Data infrastructure still in use. Strategy logic replaced by Phase 2.
